seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Amazon Bedrock Agents enable building sophisticated voice agents by combining fully managed agent orchestration with knowledge bases, action groups, and guardrails for enterprise-grade deployments. Voice agents demand sub-second latency in speech-to-text → LLM reasoning → text-to-speech pipelines, where even minor failures cascade into poor user experiences. Vex provides runtime verification to catch malformed responses, latency violations, and safety issues before they reach users.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Amazon Bedrock Agents
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents process real-time audio streams through STT (e.g., Amazon Transcribe), invoke Bedrock Agents for reasoning via action groups and knowledge bases, then synthesize responses with TTS (e.g., Amazon Polly). Bedrock Agents handle multi-turn conversations with session state, guardrails for safety, and Lambda-backed action groups for external integrations. The pipeline requires <500ms end-to-end latency, making post-processing verification critical to avoid blocking delays.

Typical flow: Audio → STT → Bedrock Agent invoke_agent() → Vex verification → TTS. Knowledge bases provide RAG for domain expertise, while guardrails filter PII/harmful content. Session state persists across turns via agent aliases and memory configurations.

risks.name: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
risks.description: Lambda functions in action groups return invalid JSON schemas, causing agent parsing failures and session crashes
risks.mitigation: Wrap invoke_agent() responses with vex.verify() using JSON schema validation specs

risks.name: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
risks.description: Poor chunking strategies cause RAG to miss key context, leading to hallucinated or irrelevant voice responses
risks.mitigation: Verify retrieval relevance scores and context completeness before TTS synthesis

risks.name: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
risks.description: Built-in guardrails miss industry-specific violations (HIPAA, finance regs), exposing compliance risks
risks.mitigation: Layer Vex custom policy checks on conversation history and agent outputs

risks.name: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
risks.description: Voice agents lose conversation context across STT chunks, causing incoherent multi-turn dialogue
risks.mitigation: Verify session state continuity and conversation coherence across agent invocations

implementation.title: Basic Bedrock Agent Invocation with Session Management
implementation.description: Invoke Bedrock Agent with persistent session state for multi-turn voice conversations

implementation.title: Real-time STT → Agent → TTS Pipeline
implementation.description: End-to-end voice pipeline with latency tracking for sub-second requirements
implementation.code:
import boto3
import time

transcribe = boto3.client('transcribe')
bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
polly = boto3.client('polly')

def voice_pipeline(audio_chunk):
    start_time = time.time()
    
    # STT
    stt_start = time.time()
    transcript = transcribe.start_stream_transcription(
        LanguageCode='en-US',
        MediaSampleRateHertz=16000,
        AudioStream=audio_chunk
    )['Transcript']
    stt_latency = time.time() - stt_start
    
    # Agent
    agent_start = time.time()
    agent_response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(
        agentId='AGENT_ID',
        agentAliasId='ALIAS_ID',
        sessionId='session-123',
        inputText=transcript
    )
    agent_latency = time.time() - agent_start
    
    # TTS
    tts_start = time.time()
    speech = polly.synthesize_speech(
        Text=agent_response['completion'],
        OutputFormat='mp3',
        VoiceId='Joanna'
    )['AudioStream'].read()
    tts_latency = time.time() - tts_start
    
    total_latency = time.time() - start_time
    print(f'Latencies: STT={stt_latency:.2f}s, Agent={agent_latency:.2f}s, TTS={tts_latency:.2f}s, Total={total_latency:.2f}s')
    
    return speech
